title: Outcomes-tracking process + annual/event-driven safety re-review cadence
context: oer-science-labs collects no telemetry and no student PII, so outcomes are measured through educator/partner self-report and the incident channel. Without a documented outcomes-tracking process and re-review cadence, guides will go stale and the zero-harm-incidents commitment cannot be maintained long-term. This task establishes the lightweight, privacy-preserving operational processes needed for sustainability. Depends on partner-017 (outcomes process is most meaningful once a partner is secured).
objective: Document and implement a privacy-preserving outcomes-tracking process and an annual/event-driven safety re-review cadence. Outcomes tracking counts distinct educators and class groups (not page views, not student-level data) via partner/educator self-report over rolling 12-month windows. Re-review is triggered annually and after any reported incident or change in official safety or curriculum guidance; stale guides are flagged. Zero-harm-incidents tracking is tied to the incident channel.
acceptanceCriteria: An outcomes-tracking process is documented covering: what is measured (distinct educators/class groups over rolling 12-month windows), how (partner/educator self-report, no telemetry), how counts are de-duplicated and recorded, and how zero-harm-incidents are tracked via the incident channel
The process explicitly states: no student-level data is collected or stored; no telemetry; no in-app tracking; estimates are recorded separately from confirmed counts

A safety re-review cadence is documented and implemented: all published guides are re-reviewed at least annually and immediately after any reported incident or change in official safety or curriculum guidance
A staleness-flagging mechanism is implemented: guides whose lastReviewed date exceeds the re-review cadence are flagged in CI or the authoring tooling

The re-review process specifies who triggers it (the safety reviewer or maintainer), what is checked (full safety block against current official guidance + any incident reports), and how the outcome is recorded (new review-log entry, updated lastReviewed date)
The outcomes and re-review process documents are committed to the repository as version-controlled artifacts, not just wiki or PR comments
output: A pull request adding the outcomes-tracking process documentation and the annual/event-driven safety re-review cadence (with staleness flagging) to the oer-science-labs repository.
